name: Glider Sim Deluxe – Soaring / Flight Simulator (Powered By Google Earth)

reviews.review_description: I've found extraordinary solace flying like a bird down the snowy, rocky face of every major mountain range worldwide. Speedwing from peaks throughout the Alps, Himalayas, and North and South America, over and over. But my first shot everyday is the Matterhorn. Seriously, track in any direction for the drop of a lifetime. Then, if time allows, I jump from a local peak, Rainier, and dive across miles of glaciers, rocky steeps, cliffs and ridge lines. This is a must have for anyone coveting wings.

reviews.review_title: The Closest Thing to Real Flight
reviews.review_description:
Glider Sim Deluxe on the Quest 3 is one of those rare VR experiences that quietly sneaks up on you and then refuses to let go. The moment you step off your first launch point and the ground falls away beneath your feet, something clicks in your brain: this isn’t just a game. It’s flight.

The sense of freedom is absolutely extraordinary. Instead of piloting a loud machine, you’re riding invisible rivers of air, searching for thermals, carving along mountain ridges, and drifting across landscapes with a calm, almost meditative rhythm. VR feels made for this kind of experience, and the Quest 3’s clarity makes the world stretch convincingly to the horizon.

What truly sets Glider Sim Deluxe apart is its realism. The physics system models wind, lift, and thermals in a way that feels alive rather than scripted. Every turn, stall, or climb responds naturally to the air around you, creating that magical “just one more flight” loop. According to the developers, the sim uses real aerodynamic calculations and dynamic weather systems to recreate authentic soaring behavior, and you can genuinely feel that attention to detail while flying.

The variety is another huge win. You’re not limited to one type of aircraft. Paragliders, hang gliders, paramotors, ultralight trikes, speed wings, and even FPV drones all offer different flying personalities. Switching between them feels like learning entirely new sports, which keeps the experience fresh for dozens of hours.

And then there’s the scale. Being able to explore real-world locations using streamed global terrain makes the game feel endlessly expansive. You can launch from mountains, coastlines, or cities across the planet, turning VR into a personal aerial travel machine. Recent updates even removed the subscription requirement for global exploration, making the experience far more accessible and generous for players.

On Quest 3 specifically, the standalone performance is impressive. Yes, compromises exist compared to high-end PC VR, but considering the headset is essentially running a full flight simulator untethered, the immersion remains remarkable. Community players frequently describe it as one of the best “free flight” experiences currently available on standalone VR hardware.

What surprised me most is how relaxing it is. Some sessions become thrilling speed runs through valleys, while others feel like floating meditation sessions where you simply watch the sun move across the sky. Few VR games manage both adrenaline and tranquility so effortlessly.

Pros

Incredible sense of presence and freedom

Deep, believable flight physics

Multiple aircraft types with unique handling

Fly virtually anywhere on Earth

Perfect showcase for Quest 3 immersion

Cons

Visual distance is limited compared to PC VR (understandable for standalone hardware)

Learning real soaring techniques takes a little patience

Final Verdict:
Glider Sim Deluxe isn’t just a flight simulator. It’s a digital escape hatch. If you’ve ever looked at birds and wondered what the air feels like beneath wings, this is about as close as VR currently gets.

Easy recommendation for Quest 3 owners.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great review Brock_MacKnight :)

reviews.review_description:
EDITED review : this app is now highly recommended with Google Maps no longer being an extra cost. 

EDITED 2 : the map quality was good for a while, maybe a month, but for the last 6 or 7 times ive played this game the google map quality has been bad, low resolution and just looks bad, ruins the immersion of flying. My internet connection is fine

EDITED 3 : Response to dev msg - Hi, I have tried all the graphics settings, have re-installed, but still the same. Its New York, was fine for a while but then just went bad and has been the same since. Sorry for the low review score, its just its unplayable now with how bad it looks.

EDITED 4 : Just tried this game on my spare Quest 3 and it ran fine, so the issue must be on my main headset. Changing the rating back to 5 stars as when its running well.its a fantastic experience. Sorry to trouble you Joey.

developer_response.body: Hi Tyke_18, what do you have the Earth Online Quality settings set at in the graphic settings? Feel free to reach out to me via Email at joeysipos@5Drealities.com or the Discord.

reviews.review_description: Iv been skydiving and speedflying for over 14 years and when the quest came out I thought about how ace it would be to have something like this, all the updates with Google earth and the flight characteristics the game just keeps improving, now my only 2 requests would be more people to fly with online and some diffrent wings, maybe the moustache, maybe some diffrent size speed wings but its great how it is already!

I was excited to be able to try launching from any paragliding location around the world...I didn't expect to be able to launch right over my own house and land in my front yard! You can literally launch anywhere that's mapped. Crazy fun! I admit, I bought the headset just to get this program, so I am just learning how to control everything, but so far I'm very happy with it. I also know that the developer is part of the flying community here and the simulation is based on actual experience. Great job!

Update 3.0.8 represents a much, MUCH better compromise between visual detail and performance!

Thanks for the quick response dev!  ; )

I really do appreciate the latest update, but I'd also be curious to see the impact of having LODs switch to a higher detail level a bit earlier for the Google Earth tiles that are closest to me, just so I can admire the scenery!

As of the latest version, I'd rather see more detail for scenery that is closer, than I feel a need to expand the overall draw distance or quality.

Looking forward for your next update!

Have a great day!

reviews.review_title: Best way to revisit places you know
reviews.review_description: Disclaimer: I just wanted a nicer way to navigate google earth, no real interest in gliders. I just pick the lightweight powered one and steer with a joystick. But it's been a great way to revisit places I used to live, work, or go to school. I'm on Quest 3 with graphics set to max detail, don't find the framerate an issue and mostly focus on the area nearest if I'm around buildings. I've tried other google earth VR apps but tbh that the glider keeps moving helps make the inherent flaws of the google earth data less noticeable or distracting. That craft is also a good speed to take things in clearly, but not so slow it takes too long to get anywhere, and unless you're pushing it or in an ultra dense area like Los Angeles seems to move at a speed the map loading details can keep up with.

I purchased this to see if I wanted to take the sport up in real life. And it honestly answered all my questions. From holding your hands above your head for an hour, to staring straight down to earth with nothing underneath you.

I highly recommend this game to anyone interested in looking into the sport. Being able to visit cities all around the world was also a wonderful experience, although at ground level some of the buildings were flat. And google earth is such that it doesn't always load until you have already passed it, but that's limited by technology.

Originaly I purchased this on the PC and tried to play on the quest 2 with link. This feature is broken and everything is a double image, so I refunded and purchased on meta and it works fine, although with reduced graphics.

If anyone is looking for new places to explore, check Dorrigo NSW Australia 😉
Flew over my old stomping grounds growing up. I've always wanted to fly but no way to do so. I always wondered. What's this look like from the sky? and now I know!!! I love it

Glider Sim had been on my wish list for a very long time, and the deciding factor for me was the integration of Google Earth without a subscription model.

Since Google Earth is now fully available to everyone with the Deluxe version, I bought the Game immediately.

And I haven't regretted it! I set the graphics settings to the highest and find the visuals very impressive for a standalone headset. 
The sound is good and the physics are believable.
And now you fly around and all over the whole World in full VR without an PC, insane! 
The VR controls are very immersive. (Apart from the Ultralight Trike, I'm still not getting along with that at all, yet) 

For the future, I would like to see a Sailplane or a small propeller Plane, as well as a stand-up Paramotor, GPS route planning, and a more lively sky, with other Aircraft and flocks of birds for example.
And an option to save mid flight would be useful. 

But for anyone who doesn't want to fire up their PC every time and has even a little interest in aviation, Glider Sim is a must-buy.
This is probably the best glider simulator with full flight modes in VR. The physics are incredible, and the fact that you can fly anywhere in the world is a total game changer.

I have been playing this particular simulator quite a long time today I woke up and the 3-D rendering in Google Earth looked a dull and ugly. I would rather pay five dollars a month for good Google Earth rendering then not have any at all when it’s supposed to work for free Sorry that you let some whiny know it all guy talk you into 
changing something because he’s spoiled whiny baby ,the game was perfect before the last update. Game was good enough

developer_response.body:
Hi there TheWindWrangler, just to clarify, no one talked me into changing anything - this was a decision I made on my own. The goal of the update was to improve performance and increase draw distance, especially on standalone hardware.

That said, I’ve also released an option so you can revert to the previous behavior if you prefer how it looked before. Simply set “Earth Online Detail” to “Very Low” and it will match the default settings from earlier versions.

In theory, the new system should actually provide better visual detail at “Medium” and above compared to older builds, but I wanted to give players the flexibility to choose what works best for them.

reviews.review_description:
I'm usually quite level headed about apps, but I can't contain myself about this one... literally visit anywhere in the world and explore in high definition (subject to internet speed)....

Even a flight over my office was fun.  Then I took a spin around Christ the Redeemer and dropped in on the Basque coastline.  A life of exploration from your living room!!!  

Many thanks Devs.

the no subscription update pushed me over the edge to give it a chance. and i love it! brilliant controls, different kites and setups- great map inclusion, fun physics and a forgiving learning curve (try some of the multiple kites& visualisation settings)

i have a blast, thanks dev(s) ! :)
I highly recommend this if you are looking for a realistic flight experience even though it’s gliding and stuff it still is tremendously fun and still feels  like the real thing

reviews.review_title: As close 2 the real thing as you can get
reviews.review_description:
If you switch off the wind particles, thermal cores etc and just fly naturally it is amazing how realistic the thermals feel when you bump them & get pushed away. 
We bought the metaquest specifically for this game and it has been so worth it! 
Another bonus with the google earth feature is you can trial flights in new locations - be warned that some areas have more 3 dimensional trees buildings etc than others so don’t take it as fact, but it is great to trial areas you haven’t flown before and explore landing site options. 
Honestly it’s so good I’m able to fly every day no matter what the weather is outside! Defo recommend!

5 stars now with free Google Earth integration and fixed bugs! Terrific chill flying/gliding joy in VR!

Google Earth + gliding/flight sim mechanics is a terrific thrill like few others in VR

pretty awesome graphics on Quest as well, including shadows

I only took out a star because I think Google API integration still needs some work. For instance, sometimes you pick a place on the map to take off from, it places you right there, but as soon as you hit Fly it just randomly throws you to another area miles away....

I don't have a problem with the subscription to access the Google map data after free first month... it's absolutely worth it

reviews.review_title: Red Bull’s got nothing on these Wiiings!
reviews.review_description:
If you’re a snowboarder/mountain biker and own a VR headset, you should definitely buy this game! It’s incredibly satisfying carving down your favourite runs (you can literally load any location in the world) but in the summer from the air. I’ve not tried multiplayer yet, but the single player experience is incredible. 

Here’s where I’ve been the last few days:


The pyramids in Giza
Las Vegas
Avoriaz 
Les Arcs

Thank you so much for such a brilliant game!

Pros:
Amazing game.
Multiple aircraft all are very realistic and fun to fly.
Multi-player is cool
Loading times are good
Game interface is easy to use
Graphics are 8/10 for being Google maps "when 3d model textures are there" 

Cons & suggestions:
Adding more aircraft and perhaps skydiving.
Occasionally in high demand locations "lots of buildings" will cause the game to crash "rare".
Sometimes when you do aggressive stunt rolls the image appears to double "or un-align" but quickly goes back to normal. 
Add smoke trails or other effects to use especially when playing multi-player would be a fun addition. 

Overall rating 8/10 
Should you buy this game?
If you want a fun or relaxing VR flying experience thats immersing, that let's you fly practically anywhere on earth and that doesn't require you additional charges then 100% yes!

There's something about flying in a major city or some small random village in Poland that makes this game so special. You might even want to see your home town from the sky. I love this game and hope to see more titles like it.
Why are the trees almost black? That looks terrible. Further the entire landscape is relatively dark, although the Quest's brightness is set to maximum.

developer_response.body: Good feedback. I darkened the trees some to fit the terrain color profile. But I can try to brighten them here with the next update. Also, if you increase the time of day / sun angle, the tree color will brighten with sun intensity.

reviews.review_description: I’m a commercial pilot and a glider pilot irl and this is the best flying game on Quest
Nice app. It would be even better if Google Street Maps was incorporated into the app.
Maybe in time SIV tutorials great fun.
This was a huge disappointment, visually speaking.

developer_response.body: Hi LaughingEagle, sorry to hear. What did you find visually disappointing just out of curiosity? Were you flying Earth Online or the Default maps?

reviews.review_description: Finally a functional flight sim- all be it with limited gliders. Still pretty good, hopefully they will keep the updates going and improve it more.

Why not an Google Earth quality Slider to change quality? 😒

Bad is a very nice game I like and love this ❤️👈

developer_response.body: Hi Extreme Begeisterung. In the latest version I have added an Earth Online / Google Earth quality slider in the graphics settings! Note that cranking up the settings may have an undesired effect on smoothness / framerate.

reviews.review_description: the game is so good

I used to fly hang-gliders a while ago (I'm over 60 now). This app is as close as you can get to the real thing. I got very nostalgic being able to fly in VR, some of the sites in Europe that I used to frequent. It nearly bought a tear to my eye. Thank you for making this app

I only fly with earth online, and there are some nice default places you can quickly select. Being able to choose from several craft is really nice, I love the hang glider but mainly the paramotor as I can stay in the air for as long as I want. I love to revisit places I have travelled to. Last week we were in Seville so now I have been flying round there, trying to land in plaza Espana. Beautiful. My only small complaints are that to regular earth online users, we really need some more options such as previously visited places etc. It's a slight hassle having to navigate and find the same spots you want to fly around using the green cross hair on the map. Also I always like to fly in the afternoon to get more thermals, so I would like to be able to select a default time to fly to save setting this each time. Thank you!

reviews.review_title: Fligt sides disappear  on Google earth?
reviews.review_description: After a month the Google erth flight sites disappeared  amother wise it is great simulator

I love this, it is amazing flying around peacefully gliding through the mountains . I would like to see some big horn sheep in there and maybe deer and definitely  a few grizzly bears ,pheasants grouse and turkeys please and thank you.Really  enjoying the experience! I wish when you  get low to the ground it would go to street view this would be awesome in the Google mode!

I wasn't sure what to expect, but I have wanted to check it out for a while.  This app is awesome, it really does a great job of simulating gliding.  The Google Earth enhancement is the way to go, as it was cool to glide around New York and Brazil.  I wish they'd had more pre-selected environments, because using the "browse earth" feature did not get me anywhere I wanted to go.  This might be a bit tough for those with motion sickness, but they have some features to alleviate that.  There is a fee for the Google features, but it's minimal if you plan to use this app a lot.  I'm not sure if it is for ME  but I am definitely impressed with it.

“As a paraglider pilot, I can say this game/simulator is the closest you can get to the real thing. It’s amazing how realistically you can catch thermals and experience lift. If you’re looking to get into paragliding, this is a great way to experience a digital version of it. I totally recommend it to anyone who loves paragliding!”
It doesn't let me take off it just flips me over and when I turn it doesn't but maybe I'll figure it out some day

developer_response.body: Hi Bunny11, it should be fairly easy to get started flying. Just launch your glider and lean forward or use trigger for throttle. Are you having issues taking off on the default maps, or Earth Online? Also make sure you did not uncheck the "Auto Inflate Canopy" in the settings, as that will make taking off much more difficult...

reviews.review_title: One of my favorites!!!  Changed my life!
reviews.review_description:
I love this game!!!!! I play it almost daily. It literally got me into paramotoring and I completed my training school this past summer. The instructor was amazed at my flight and landing skills and I 100% attribute it to this game.  It's really therapeutic just soaring around in the wind in the mountains with the birds!!

***Big thanks to Joey at 5D realities for his relentless updating of the game!!**
 Looking forward to the new maps/Earth online update!!!!
The physics and graphics are wonderful.  Just tried the Google maps scenery and it exceeded my expectations, so much to explore!!

I have over 30 years of experience as a hang glider pilot and previously owned the first HG simulator from Wilco about 20 years ago. After trying out various alternatives, I am thoroughly impressed with the realism of the Quest 3 Standalone version simulator. The air texture and wing behavior feel incredibly authentic, mimicking the response of a real novice-level Hqng Glider at different speeds. I appreciate the convenience of having the glider trimmed to best glide speed, allowing for a smooth transition from the speed bar to the joysticks on the controllers. The multiplayer option has allowed me to share the sky and experiences with other users through convenient VHF  radio communication.  Sharing a thermal and putting into practice the flying traffic rules  gives it a sense of realism that I had only experienced in real life flights. This simulator is a valuable tool for improving overall technique, and I highly recommend it to anyone interested in free flying. I acknowledge that it is still a work in progress, but even in its current state, it stands out as the best choice in the market.

reviews.review_title: Almost great, except for graphic shimmer
reviews.review_description: I love sims even more than games.  Always wanted to try hang gliding, and here's my chance to learn about it in VR -- something I'd never be able to do IRL.  This is a great flying sim -- would have given it 5 stars except for one thing:  even on my Quest 3, the terrain graphics are awfully shimmery -- it gives me a headache after half an hour or so.  I have tried turning on and off various graphics settings but nothing helps.  Is this a case where QGO would help?  Anyway, kudos to the devs for an excellent sim, great tutorial, nice big play areas... but please can we make the landscape less shimmery?  Oh BTW forgot to say, FPV drone mode is an awesome extra!

developer_response.body: Hi Tazling. Be sure to check out the latest update. Tree shimmer should be greatly reduced to almost removed ;)

reviews.review_description: Would be cool to use the power of the new headset and unlock PCVR like graphics. Also the controllers need an update visually, the speedbar is not working. All in all a good simulator but can be even better!

I have been flying real paragliders for several years and am used to controlling a wing for several hours at a stretch in real life. This simulation is the best paragliding simulation I know. The graphics are sufficiently good. The simulation is great! I played it in a real harness and it's actually fantastic VR in Most ways...
...... Now for the but:
This game on the MetaQuest 3 makes me incredibly nauseous. I can play VR first-person shooters for hours and everything is fine. But this simulator makes my stomach turn after just 5 minutes in simulatet thermal conditions.

Pleased find a way o geti rid of this motion sickness effect.


OK! Thanks I tried it an this "Keep Head leveled"  Option and flying without my real harness just sitting in a chair works good.....
Now it it is just the best paragliding simulaion I know. I did a 60 minutes flight today without any motion sickness = 5 stars now!

fantastic experience!!! I was to quick to put 4 stars in a very very early access. Deserves the 5. Super relaxing and fun and now with the drone option included it makes it thrilling and exciting also. I keep coming back to it. 
New map in Switcherland, really fun. I hope for more maps and challenges in the future, but mostly maps, maybe city maps even if they are not real cities, for the thrill of speed gliding between buildings (maybe a base jump mode? 🤷‍♂️asking is free…  😅) and run the drone among them… changed my 4 to 5 stars, kudos to the devs for an amazing work.The physics are incredible. So worth it!

Had one flight had to have a lie down felt sick 
I love it 
Seems real on the meta quest 2 
Need Practice i suppose get used to it worth the £££’s

reviews.review_title: Don't waste your money on Oculus version
reviews.review_description:
I bought the Applabs version which installs directly to the Oculus Quest 2 and boy what a waste of money! The video settings are scaled all the way down and greyed out so they cannot be adjusted. The Steam version for PC, on the other hand, will allow you to adjust the graphics based on your PC's specs (like 99% of all games out there). IF THEY HAD MADE THIS KNOWN on the sales literature, I never would have bought it! The game is terrible with the graphics turned down all the way. It's like I went back in time 20 years and I'm playing a game on my first PC with a Pentium 2 cpu, my 8 meg video card, and 256 megs of ram. I tried getting a refund and it is not allowed! Even though I played it for far less than the 2 hour window.
What a ripoff.

developer_response.body: Please check out the latest update Early Access 2.0 (July 20, 2023). The version standalone Quest version now updated to the same version as Steam and includes improved graphics and the Swiss Alps map! ;)

reviews.review_description: I’ve been paragliding for a few years now and specially bought a Quest 2 after trying it out on a friend’s vr setup using Steam. I wasn’t too concerned about graphic quality, more with the dynamics of the paraglider. It is pretty good, and the low graphics don’t hinder me. The controls are pretty good, especially the head lean and brake steering. Big ears is there after a rear pull on the left joystick. I have no idea why left & right also operate the brakes, I can only imagine for the paramotor rig. The real disappointment at the moment is Steam has 2 maps, one In Switzerland, whereas Meta only has the one. Also, there is no multiplayer on currently on Quest. I looking forward to other maps to be released for the Quest, and multiplayer. Everything considered, a great sim and is likely to get even better.

My 25 Dollars are well spent for a really good simulation.  The gliding and everything feels like the real thing. Only critic i have are the challenges. There are only 3 in fact.  And they are the reason i am comming back and back just to get a better overall score. Please implement more in one of the next updates.  Something like a carrer mode would be awsome.

I’m a skydiver, paraglider and Paramotor pilot with lots of  jumps and glider hours. This game is very cool. I would almost call it a simulator. But you need to tweak the physics a bit more.  For example when you push the speedbar the glider continues to dive for 2 sec it doesn’t do that irl either. When flying smaller wings the toggle input it’s to sluggish and the turns are hard to stop. The glide ratio on the wings are some over the top. That’s not so irl.
You can do helicopters but not infinity tumble or fall into the glider very hard to do 
acro. Otherwise nice job! Been missing this.
You really got the visuals of flying.

As a P2 rated paragliding pilot, I’ll have to admit this is very spot on with the real thing. It absolutely is relatable to the physics of the real thing (without the g’s or feeling the rush of wind).
I will 100% share this with fellow pilots! Especially those who haven’t flown and want a taste and understanding of paragliding to see if they enjoy it before having to throw down cash for instructor training (would not recommend attempting real life flying without having a certified instructor to guide you). 
A little extra feedback for the GOAT dev(s) who created this, please keep it up!
-would be amazing to see more flying sites, such as jackson hole Wyoming, pine mountain Oregon #1 IMO, top of the mountain Utah, south mountain Arizona, coastal areas, or some international ones that are in the alps, turkey, etc. Not sure if it’s possible, but maybe could use google earth to get topo/imagery references as a basis to speed up dev on new maps
-would be great to be able to use the vario, maybe be able to switch between the first view and a map view w/ compass and flight path heading
-after landing, being able to see a flight log showing the flight path on a 2d map and tracking to watch path in 3d via replay, fast forward, rewind, play in slo mo, pause, stop, etc 
- showing vario stats with replay
-Additional challenges - “SIV Clinic”, spirals, stalls, wingovers, the more the better
-Options to set gust filters (higher than base speed), variable wind speed filters, wind direction filters
-Adding a reserve (to practice throwing when in a stall) - reach under handle by seat and pull to throw

Overall, very impressed, thanks for providing a way to share the world of paragliding to those who may not otherwise been able to access and pursue. Great work, looking forward to future updates!

To the developers, the gameplay and physics is good, the only problem is that it needs to be a more real life experience with the graphics. Please either add the ability to tweak the graphics in game (as it does have this feature but you cannot use on the quest) or please have a high resolution texture update at some point. When you look down it kind of looks flat with a little 3d look to it and the textures seem to shimmer about.

That being said, if the graphics was improved, it would really change the experience by 10 fold.

I know this game is still in early stages and still being worked on so sorry if the comment was a little harsh.

developer_response.body: I wish I could enable them. But please note that this is a limitation of the Quest hardware. Which is basically the same as a cell phone... To give you perspective. I am limited to 2.5 GB of memory usage. And the game uses like 2.4GB on the Quest. Any higher resolution and the game will not run. If you want high resolution be sure to check out the PC version on Steam. A gaming PC is 100x more powerful and you can crank all those settings up to your hearts desire ;) High resolution, dense grass, real time shadows... Its all there in the PC version.

reviews.review_description:
I’m a reasonably experienced paraglider pilot, about 60 hours in total. Through life circumstances I gave up paragliding about nine years ago, but always missed it. I was a little dubious when I saw this on app lab, but I’ve just had a whale of a time hopping between thermals and scratching around for lift. At one point I could swear I could feel that glider pressure through the lines! Thank you 😁😁😁 

(Will there be more sites in the future?)

developer_response.body: Glad you enjoyed. Especially coming from a paragliding pilot. Yes, I hope to add 2 more scenery locations in the base pack. But it really depends if I can make enough sales to continue development here... So please share with all your friends and get the word out :)

reviews.review_description:
First off, I want to say that I am incredibly impressed that a sim like this can run standalone on the quest. Scratching around trying to find some lift or throwing the wing around in aerobatics is seriously fun and (to a non paraglider pilot at least) the physics seem to be perfectly adequate to get a taste for the sport. Having the ability to collapse the wing is a very nice touch for added realism though I can't comment on its accuracy.

A bit of feedback for the developer:

1. While I'm not a paraglider pilot, I am a sailplane pilot so I've got a bit of experience with ridge lift and thermals IRL. It feels like the ridge lift should extend higher off the top of the hill, for example, at our local site we'll commonly see ridge lift up to 1000ft above the top of the ridge and our ridge is nowhere near as nice as the ones in game. Centring yourself in a thermal feels spot on though, it would be good if they were generated based on terrain rather than randomly though.

2. While this doesn't really affect the general sim experience, it would be nice if the wing started on the ground when taking off.

3. Some steeper terrain and bigger mountains would go a long way to making the speed wing more usable.

Look good for beta but need lots of improvement. First is less use vibration on controllers, it’s take lots of power and decrease battery dramatically. 
Need to add calibration for hands position on brakes, cos now it’s to high for me. 
More accuracy flight control and possibility to turn off visualization of wind end thermals. 
Hope all this things will be improvement to release of this simulator. But any case it’s an amazing and wait to try it with my harness 😊
😳 the developers responses "it's not supposed to be fun it's supposed to be real !" okay well it's still not fun at all and if this is what it is like in real life I see why it is not more popular

developer_response.body: Advice noted. More fun elements. I hope to add bigger steeper terrains in the future, that way you can do speed runs down steep canyons under bridges and so forth, or perhaps something like wingsuits might be possible in the future. Thanks for giving it a shot none the less :)

reviews.review_description: I really feel like I’m flying on a paraglider! Great job on physics and environment. It would be nice if they added a tutorial to help get started faster! Overall it’s awesome!
I fly the real thing, and this sim nails it! Buy it! Tell your friends to buy it! Let the developers know it's worth improving on.

I don’t paraglide but hope to start soon. Got this app to scratch the itch until I can afford the real deal. I have loved watching YouTube videos to learn techniques then practicing them in VR. This simulator reacts exactly how they say it would.
Not bad. Wish there were more places to fly, though. Besides that, it's very realistic.

developer_response.body: Hi K_V14, Be sure to check out the latest update. Earth Online - World Streaming Service Powered by Google Earth Photorealistic tiles is now live within Glider Sim! You can now fly anywhere in the world!

reviews.review_description: Really useful, i like it also share how easy it is fly and, how u should thermal etc. I really love if possible Ölydeniz map🙏
This is absolutely fantastic! It won't be long until all Paramotor schools use this as a simulator! Thank you so much

This is one of the best simulators I have ever flown. It is as close to paragliding as you can get without strapping on a paraglider and running off the side of a mountain. This simulator allows for long cross-country flights (I have flown for an hour and a half on one recent flight) or gliding along ridge fronts. You can challenge your skills in high wind or soar on the thermals on a quiet day. This simulator lets me truly fly by the seat of my pants, no one shooting at me, no missions, just flying where I want with success measured by my level of happiness and enjoyment. I recommend this simulator to those of us out there who are looking for barebones flying, no frills and total control of where you go and how you fly.
